description_long: This object is a highly stylized representation of a crustacean, likely inspired by a shrimp or prawn, with an extremely minimalistic and abstract design. The body is reduced to a pair of long, slender, intersecting rods that form a V-shape, with additional rods extending outward, possibly representing legs or antennae. The structure is delicate and spindly, with no visible segmentation or detailed anatomical features such as eyes, mouthparts, or claws. The rods appear metallic, with a smooth, slightly reflective surface and a muted, silvery color. The overall form is open and airy, with significant negative space between the elements, giving it a lightweight and almost skeletal appearance. This object would stand out as a modern art piece or decorative element in an urban environment, rather than a realistic depiction of a crustacean.
description: A minimalistic, abstract metallic crustacean form composed of intersecting slender rods, evoking the shape of a shrimp or prawn. The structure is airy, skeletal, and visually striking with a silvery finish.
description_view_0: The front view shows the intersecting rods forming a symmetrical V-shape, with two long rods extending upward and outward, and two more diverging at the base.
description_view_1: From the side, only a single rod is clearly visible, emphasizing the thinness and linearity of the structure.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the rods diverging outward, with the open V-shape most prominent and no central body mass.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors view 1, again showing a single rod and highlighting the object's minimal profile.
